description_long: This is a large, ancient-style ceramic vase with a wide, rounded body, a narrow neck, and two robust handles extending from the shoulder to the rim. The surface is adorned with intricate black-figure artwork, depicting mythological scenes with human and animal figures, including chariots and warriors, characteristic of Greek pottery. The neck and upper shoulder feature floral and geometric motifs, while the lower body is decorated with swirling patterns and stylized palmettes. The rim and base are slightly flared, with the interior of the mouth appearing dark and unglazed. The vase's coloration is primarily a warm terracotta with black painted details, and the handles show signs of wear. This object exudes a sense of historical craftsmanship and would serve as a striking decorative piece in a public or cultural setting.
description: A large, terracotta Greek-style vase with black-figure mythological scenes, floral motifs, and two sturdy handles. The surface is richly decorated with intricate patterns and classical artwork.
description_view_0: Front view shows a detailed chariot scene with horses and warriors, highlighting the main narrative artwork and both handles.
description_view_1: Side view focuses on one handle and the swirling geometric patterns, with less emphasis on the narrative figures.
description_view_2: Opposite side displays a group of standing human figures in black-figure style, with both handles visible.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, emphasizing the handle and decorative scrollwork, with minimal narrative imagery.
